story: Bruce was a sailor who hunted for meaning on black water. That morning, the harbor‑master ran up a gale pennant at the pier and warned that no small boats should go out. Soon after, Bruce staggered into my bar, begged three glasses of water, squared up to a phantom foe, and finally gasped, "I have to go, Brutish. I can’t breathe." He hurried to the door, sunlight bursting on stone, and from the street shouted, "I am not a man! I am a fish! I belong to the sea!" It was the last time I heard him.
implausible_knowledge: the harbor‑master ran up a gale pennant at the pier and warned that no small boats should go out
question: Right after Bruce runs out declaring he belongs to the sea, what will Brutish most plausibly do?
answer_with_knowledge: Run to the pier to stop him from launching and warn the harbor‑master.
answer_without_knowledge: Let Bruce go and return to tending the bar.
